rulings.Case Title: RODRICK BRECKLER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 15, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Unique Electronic Transfer & Storage, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues one, four and five (negligence, loss of consortium and contractor liability) and granted as to issues two, three, and six (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. Defendant did not present sufficient evidence to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Breckler was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s contention interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Mr. Breckler preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar and Union Bank. Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit to their claims for strict liability and false representation and their request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 15, 2011, line 2. Plaintiff George Patterson, Sherley Patterson's Demurrer To Answer To Complaint is off calendar. Duplicate entry. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 15, 2011, line 2. Plaintiffs¿ demurrer to defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Co.¿s answer to plaintiffs¿ complaint will be heard at 9:15am in Department 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Plaintiffs¿ demurrer is overruled as to the 26 affirmative defenses to which they demurred. Given the lack of factual specificity of the allegations in the complaint against defendant, it is not reasonable to expect defendant to plead its affirmative defenses with greater factual specificity. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 15, 2011, line 2. Defendant Dow Chemical¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s complaint is continued on the court¿s own motion to July 20. Assuming without deciding that defendant¿s demurrer is untimely, the court has the discretion to decide it on its merits. McAllister v. County of Monterey, 147 Cal. App. 4th 253, 281. Since plaintiffs did not file a substantive opposition, the court is continuing the hearing to allow plaintiffs to do so, if they wish. Plaintiff¿s opposition, if any, must be filed and served by July 6 at 4:00pm. Defendant¿s reply, if any, must be filed and served by July 13 at 4:00pm. = (503/TLJ)
